'''Bormter''' was a [[Wild Space/Legends|Wild Space]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] that was [[Homeworld/Legends|home]] to the four-[[Limb/Legends|armed]] [[Reptile/Legends|reptiloid]] [[Bormterran]] [[Species/Legends|species]]. Following the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Delurin]] broke with the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] and declared himself a [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]], conquering Bormter with his [[Warship/Legends|warship]], the ''[[Galaxy Dragon]]''. Delurin used Bormter as a base and recruited Bormterrans to serve in the [[Dragon trooper (Bormterran)|dragon trooper]] military unit.

==Description==
Bormter was a terrestrial [[Planet/Legends|planet]] occupying the fifth [[Orbit/Legends|orbital]] position within the [[Svekk system]]<ref name="Galaxy Dragon" /> of [[Wild Space/Legends|Wild Space]].<ref name="EAOC" /> The planet lay in one of the frontier areas of Wild [[Realspace/Legends|Space]].<ref name="Galaxy Dragon" />

==History==
[[File:Bormterran.jpg|thumb|right|225px|Bormterrans were native to Bormter and served as dragon troopers for Warlord Delurin.]]
Shortly after the [[Death/Legends|death]] of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Galactic Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] during the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]]<ref name="Galaxy Dragon" /> in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]],<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Delurin]] split from the Empire, declaring himself [[Warlord of Wild Space]]. His first action as a [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]] was to use the prototype [[Dragon-class Heavy Cruiser|''Dragon''-class Heavy Cruiser]] ''[[Galaxy Dragon]]'', manned by a crew of loyal supporters, to conquer Bormter.<ref name="Galaxy Dragon" />

The planet had all the necessary resources for Delurin to establish a power base for an [[Delurin's empire|empire]] that he proceeded to carve out in Wild Space, as well as a supply point for the ''Galaxy Dragon'' and its crew. Delurin also recruited the planet's native [[Bormterran]]s to serve as the [[Dragon trooper (Bormterran)|dragon troopers]], an elite military unit ultimately loyal to the warlord.<ref name="Galaxy Dragon" />

==Inhabitants==
Bormter was the [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of the Bormterrans, a [[Species/Legends|species]] of tall, four-[[Limb/Legends|armed]] [[Reptile/Legends|reptiloids]] that, although technologically primitive, were fierce melee fighters.<ref name="Galaxy Dragon" />

==Behind the scenes==
Bormter was first mentioned in "[[The Galaxy Dragon and crew|The ''Galaxy Dragon'' and crew]]," an article written by [[Bill Slavicsek]] as a supplement for [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'' and published in [[Dragon Magazine 200|''Dragon Magazine'' 200]] in [[December]] [[1993]]. Since that article was released outside of the [[Lucas Licensing]] process,<ref name="Galaxy Dragon" /> it was considered non-[[canon]]ical with respect to the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity.<ref name="UnlicensedStatus">{{UnlicensedStatus}}</ref> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Svekk system, and therefore Bormter, in grid square H-14.<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref>
